alabama.name: Alabama
alabama.taxLevyNote: IRS uses its own formula based on filing status and dependents. Alabama follows federal guidelines for state tax levies.
alabama.specialNotes: Alabama follows federal garnishment limits under the CCPA. No additional state protections beyond federal law.
alabama.metaTitle: Alabama Wage Garnishment Calculator | AL Garnishment Laws & Limits
alabama.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your Alabama paycheck can be legally garnished. Free calculator covers child support, consumer debt, student loans, and tax levies under AL law.
alabama.introContent: Alabama follows the federal Consumer Credit Protection Act (CCPA) for wage garnishment limits, meaning creditors can garnish up to 25% of your disposable earnings or the amount by which your weekly disposable earnings exceed 30 times the federal minimum wage, whichever is less. Alabama does not provide additional protections beyond federal law, making it one of the states that adheres strictly to the federal baseline.
keyProtections: Federal CCPA limits apply: 25% of disposable earnings or amount exceeding 30x minimum wage
Child support garnishment: 50-65% depending on circumstances
Head of household has no additional state-level protection
Social Security benefits are generally exempt from garnishment

alaska.name: Alaska
alaska.taxLevyNote: Alaska has no state income tax. Federal IRS levies use their own formula.
alaska.specialNotes: Alaska follows federal garnishment limits. The state has no income tax, which may affect disposable earnings calculations. Alaska's higher minimum wage does not change the federal garnishment calculation.
alaska.metaTitle: Alaska Wage Garnishment Calculator | AK Garnishment Laws & Limits
alaska.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your Alaska paycheck can be legally garnished. Free calculator covers child support, consumer debt, student loans, and tax levies under AK law.
alaska.introContent: Alaska adheres to federal wage garnishment limits under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. While Alaska has a higher state minimum wage than the federal level, garnishment calculations still use the federal minimum wage of $7.25 per hour. Notably, Alaska has no state income tax, which can result in higher disposable earnings and potentially higher garnishment amounts.
keyProtections: Federal CCPA limits apply: 25% of disposable earnings
No state income tax — disposable earnings may be higher
Alaska Permanent Fund dividends may be subject to garnishment for child support
Federal student loan garnishment capped at 15% of disposable earnings

arizona.name: Arizona
arizona.taxLevyNote: Arizona follows federal guidelines for tax levies. State tax levies are handled by the Arizona Department of Revenue.
arizona.specialNotes: Arizona follows federal CCPA limits for consumer debt garnishment. The state has specific procedures for garnishment writs that must be followed by employers.
arizona.metaTitle: Arizona Wage Garnishment Calculator | AZ Garnishment Laws & Limits
arizona.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your Arizona paycheck can be legally garnished. Free calculator covers child support, consumer debt, student loans, and tax levies under AZ law.
arizona.introContent: Arizona follows the federal Consumer Credit Protection Act for wage garnishment limits on consumer debts. Creditors must obtain a court judgment before garnishing wages in Arizona, and the garnishment is limited to 25% of disposable earnings or the amount exceeding 30 times the federal minimum wage. Arizona's garnishment procedures require specific court filings and employer compliance timelines.
keyProtections: Federal CCPA limits apply for consumer debt
Creditors must obtain a court judgment before garnishing wages
Employers must respond to garnishment writs within 10 business days
Certain income sources like Social Security are exempt

arkansas.name: Arkansas
arkansas.taxLevyNote: Arkansas Department of Finance and Administration can levy wages for state tax debts.
arkansas.specialNotes: Arkansas follows federal CCPA limits. The state constitution provides some additional protections for wages of married persons.
arkansas.metaTitle: Arkansas Wage Garnishment Calculator | AR Garnishment Laws & Limits
arkansas.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your Arkansas paycheck can be legally garnished. Free calculator covers child support, consumer debt, student loans, and tax levies under AR law.
arkansas.introContent: Arkansas follows the federal Consumer Credit Protection Act for wage garnishment limits. The state allows garnishment of up to 25% of disposable earnings for consumer debts. Arkansas has constitutional provisions that provide some additional protections, particularly for the wages of married individuals, though these protections have been narrowed by court interpretations over the years.
keyProtections: Federal CCPA limits apply: 25% of disposable earnings
Constitutional protections for wages of married persons
Court judgment required before wage garnishment
60-day waiting period after judgment before garnishment can begin

california.name: California
california.taxLevyNote: California Franchise Tax Board can garnish up to 25% of disposable earnings for state tax debts without a court order.
california.specialNotes: California provides stronger protections than federal law. The state protects 40 times the state minimum wage (not federal), which is significantly more protective given California's $16.90/hr minimum wage. This means $676/week is protected in California vs. $217.50 under federal law.
california.metaTitle: California Wage Garnishment Calculator | CA Garnishment Laws & Limits
california.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your California paycheck can be legally garnished. CA law protects more of your income than federal law. Free calculator for all garnishment types.
california.introContent: California provides significantly stronger wage garnishment protections than federal law. While federal law protects earnings up to 30 times the federal minimum wage ($217.50/week), California protects earnings up to 40 times the California state minimum wage ($16.90/hr), meaning $676 per week is shielded from garnishment. This makes California one of the most protective states in the nation for wage earners facing garnishment.
keyProtections: 40x state minimum wage protected ($676/week) — far exceeds federal protection
Maximum garnishment is the lesser of 25% or amount exceeding 40x state minimum wage
Earnings Withholding Order required for most garnishments
Additional protections for low-income earners under state law

colorado.name: Colorado
colorado.taxLevyNote: Colorado Department of Revenue can levy wages for state tax debts.
colorado.specialNotes: Colorado protects 40 times the state minimum wage, providing stronger protection than federal law. With Colorado's minimum wage of $15.16/hr, this protects $606.40/week.
colorado.metaTitle: Colorado Wage Garnishment Calculator | CO Garnishment Laws & Limits
colorado.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your Colorado paycheck can be legally garnished. CO law protects 40x the state minimum wage. Free calculator for all garnishment types.
colorado.introContent: Colorado offers enhanced wage garnishment protections compared to federal law. The state protects 40 times the Colorado minimum wage from garnishment, which at $15.16 per hour means $606.40 per week is shielded. This is substantially more than the federal protection of $217.50 per week. Colorado's approach ensures that workers earning closer to minimum wage retain a larger portion of their earnings.
keyProtections: 40x state minimum wage protected ($606.40/week)
Stronger protection than federal CCPA baseline
Continuing garnishment requires periodic court review

connecticut.specialNotes: Connecticut protects 40 times the state or federal minimum wage (whichever is higher). With Connecticut's $16.94/hr minimum wage, $677.60/week is protected.

connecticut.introContent: Connecticut provides robust wage garnishment protections, shielding 40 times the higher of the state or federal minimum wage from creditors. With Connecticut's minimum wage at $16.94 per hour, this means $677.60 per week is protected from garnishment — more than triple the federal protection. Connecticut is among the most protective states for wage earners facing consumer debt garnishment.
keyProtections: 40x the higher of state or federal minimum wage protected ($677.60/week)
Among the most protective states in the nation

delaware.name: Delaware
delaware.taxLevyNote: Delaware Division of Revenue can levy wages for state tax debts.
delaware.specialNotes: Delaware limits consumer debt garnishment to 15% of disposable earnings, which is more protective than the federal 25% limit.

While federal law allows up to 25% of disposable earnings to be garnished, Delaware caps consumer debt garnishment at just 15% of disposable earnings. This makes Delaware one of the more protective states for workers facing wage garnishment from credit card debt, medical bills, and other consumer obligations.

florida.name: Florida
florida.taxLevyNote: Florida has no state income tax. Federal IRS levies use their own formula.
florida.specialNotes: Florida provides a powerful head of household exemption. If you qualify as head of household (providing more than 50% of support for a dependent), your wages are completely exempt from garnishment for consumer debts up to $750/week. Above that threshold, you must consent to garnishment.
florida.metaTitle: Florida Wage Garnishment Calculator | FL Head of Household Exemption
florida.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your Florida paycheck can be legally garnished. FL's head of household exemption can protect 100% of your wages. Free calculator.
florida.introContent: Florida offers one of the strongest wage garnishment protections in the country through its head of household exemption. If you provide more than half the support for a child or other dependent, your wages may be completely exempt from garnishment for consumer debts if you earn $750 or less per week. Even above that threshold, a head of household must provide written consent for garnishment. Florida also has no state income tax, which affects disposable earnings calculations.
keyProtections: Head of household exemption can protect 100% of wages up to $750/week
Above $750/week, head of household must consent to garnishment

new-mexico.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your New Mexico paycheck can be legally garnished. NM protects 40x state minimum wage. Free calculator for all garnishment types.
new-mexico.introContent: New Mexico provides enhanced wage garnishment protections compared to federal law. The state protects 40 times the New Mexico minimum wage from garnishment. With the state minimum wage at $12.00 per hour, this means $480 per week is protected — more than double the federal protection of $217.50 per week.
keyProtections: 40x state minimum wage protected ($480/week)
Stronger protection than federal CCPA baseline
Court judgment required for consumer debt garnishment
Specific exemption procedures available

new-york.name: New York
new-york.taxLevyNote: New York State Department of Taxation and Finance can levy wages for state tax debts.
new-york.specialNotes: New York provides very strong protections. Garnishment is limited to 10% of gross wages or 25% of disposable earnings, whichever is less. Additionally, if disposable earnings are less than 30 times the applicable state minimum wage, no garnishment is allowed at all. New York's 2026 minimum wage is regional: $17.00/hr in New York City, Long Island, and Westchester (protecting $510/week), and $16.00/hr in the rest of the state (protecting $480/week). This calculator uses the $17.00 downstate rate.
new-york.metaTitle: New York Wage Garnishment Calculator | NY Garnishment Laws & Limits
new-york.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your New York paycheck can be legally garnished. NY limits garnishment to 10% of gross wages. Free calculator for all garnishment types.
new-york.introContent: New York provides some of the strongest wage garnishment protections in the nation. The state limits consumer debt garnishment to the lesser of 10% of gross wages or 25% of disposable earnings. Furthermore, if your weekly disposable earnings fall below 30 times the applicable New York minimum wage, no consumer debt garnishment is permitted at all. New York's minimum wage is regional in 2026: $17.00 per hour in New York City, Long Island, and Westchester County, which protects $510 per week, and $16.00 per hour in the remainder of the state, which protects $480 per week — either way, far above the federal floor of $217.50. Collection is done through an Income Execution under CPLR §5231, and only one may run against your wages at a time.
keyProtections: Garnishment limited to lesser of 10% gross or 25% disposable
No garnishment if disposable earnings below 30x state minimum wage ($510/week downstate, $480/week upstate)
Among the strongest protections in the nation
Income Execution required (not standard garnishment)
new-york.consumerDebtLimitText: Lesser of 10% of gross wages or 25% of disposable earnings

north-carolina.name: North Carolina
north-carolina.taxLevyNote: North Carolina Department of Revenue can levy wages for state tax debts.
north-carolina.specialNotes: North Carolina generally does not allow wage garnishment for consumer debts. Wages can only be garnished for child support, taxes, and student loans. This makes NC one of the most protective states.
north-carolina.metaTitle: North Carolina Wage Garnishment Calculator | NC Garnishment Laws & Limits
north-carolina.metaDescription: Calculate how much of your North Carolina paycheck can be legally garnished. NC generally prohibits garnishment for consumer debts. Free calculator.
north-carolina.introContent: North Carolina is one of the most protective states in the nation when it comes to wage garnishment. The state generally prohibits wage garnishment for consumer debts such as credit card debt, medical bills, and personal loans. Wages can only be garnished in North Carolina for child support, federal and state taxes, and federal student loans. This makes North Carolina an exceptionally favorable state for workers facing consumer debt issues.
keyProtections: No wage garnishment allowed for consumer debts
Only child support, taxes, and student loans can trigger garnishment
One of the most protective states in the nation

north-carolina.consumerDebtLimitText: Wage garnishment for consumer debts is generally prohibited under North Carolina law

ohio.introContent: Ohio follows federal wage garnishment limits under the Consumer Credit Protection Act but provides an additional minimum exemption. Ohio law requires that at least $425.00 per week of personal earnings be exempt from garnishment, which is nearly double the federal protection of $217.50 per week. This provides significant additional protection for Ohio workers.

oklahoma.name: Oklahoma

oklahoma.introContent: Oklahoma follows the federal Consumer Credit Protection Act for wage garnishment limits. The state provides that 75% of earnings earned in the 90 days prior to garnishment are exempt, meaning up to 25% can be garnished for consumer debts. Oklahoma requires creditors to obtain a court judgment before garnishing wages.

texas.introContent: Texas is one of the most protective states in the nation when it comes to wage garnishment. The Texas Constitution and state law completely prohibit wage garnishment for consumer debts, including credit card debt, medical bills, and personal loans. Wages can only be garnished in Texas for child support, spousal maintenance, federal and state taxes, and federal student loans. Combined with no state income tax, Texas provides exceptional protection for workers' wages.
